response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer (~15 years) with strong full-stack, high-performance, and concurrent-systems experience, but the role's central requirement—8–12 years of C++ as a primary language—is not met. Their only professional C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis project; the bulk of their career is in DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in C++, IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle, industrial automation protocols, or Azure. Despite genuinely relevant transferable skills (low-latency, multi-threaded, event-driven systems), the C++ and industrial-cybersecurity gaps make this a weak match for a senior C++ platform role.
strengths: Deep experience with high-performance, low-latency, concurrent systems (async I/O, fibers, millions of requests/sec)
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling
Versatile, full-stack engineer with proven event-driven platform and architecture experience
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ (a decade ago); not a core, current language for the candidate
No exposure to MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3)
No Azure cloud or demonstrated IoT/industrial-automation domain background
